title: System and method for modifying virtual objects in a virtual environment in response to user interactions
abstract: The methods, systems, techniques, and components described herein ailow interaction volumes of virtual objects in a virtual environment, such as a Virtual Reality (VR) environment or Augmented Reality (AR) environment, to be modified based on user interactions taken on virtual frames created for those virtual objects. A user interaction element of a virtual frame may receive a user interaction. The user interaction may comprise one or more instructions to modify the size, shape, or other visual property of the virtual object. As an example, the user interaction may comprise one or more instructions to change a size of the virtual object while maintaining a scale of the virtual object, in response to the user interaction, visual properties of the virtual frame and/or the virtual object may be modified, interaction volumes of component elements of the virtual frame as well as interaction volumes of the virtual object may be modified in response to the user interaction.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of modifying spatial dimensions of a virtual object in a virtual environment in response to a user interaction, the method comprising: obtaining a virtual object having a virtual frame, the virtual frame including an: a frame boundary, the frame boundary containing an interaction volume defining an area of interactivity related to the virtual object; and a modification element associated with at least a portion of the virtual object, the modification element used to modify a rendering of the virtual object in the virtual environment; receiving the user interaction on the modification element, the user interaction corresponding to a request to modify the rendering of the virtual object in the virtual environment; modifying, in accordance with the request: the spatial dimensions of the one or more of the primitive virtual elements of the frame boundary of the virtual frame; and the rendering of the virtual object. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the virtual frame is associated with a polygon and the virtual frame is configured to the shape of the polygon. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the polygon is a rectangle. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein: the modification element comprises opposite ends of the virtual frame; and the user interaction corresponds to a request to move the opposite ends along an axis separating the opposite ends. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the user interaction comprises an inward user interaction moving the opposite ends toward one another, or an outward user interaction moving the opposite ends away from one another. 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein: the frame boundary is composed of one or more primitive virtual elements, the one or more primitive virtual elements having spatial dimensions and being associated with at least a portion of an interaction volume of the virtual object. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein: the modification element comprises a corner of the virtual frame, the corner coupling two of the line primitive elements to one another; and the user interaction corresponds to a request to move the corner along an axis coupling the corner to a center of the virtual frame. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the user interaction corresponds to an inward user interaction moving the corner toward a center of the virtual frame, or an outward user interaction moving the corner away from the center of the virtual frame. 9. The method of claim 7, further comprising: identifying a distance between the corner and a location of the user interaction; assigning a virtual tension to the location of the user interaction based on the distance; and modifying the spatial dimensions of the one or more primitive virtual elements based on the virtual force. 10. The method of claim 6, wherein: the modification element comprises a corner of the virtual frame, the corner coupling two of the line primitive elements to one another; and the user interaction corresponds to a request to resize the virtual frame along a pivot point at a center of the virtual frame. 1 1. The method of claim 1 Q, wherein rescaling the virtual frame comprises maintaining a scale of the virtual frame. 12. The method of claim 6, wherein: the modification element comprises an edge of the virtual frame, the edge corresponding to one of the line primitive elements; and the user interaction corresponds to a translation gesture moving the virtual frame from a first anchor point in the virtual environment to a second anchor point in the virtual environment. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the virtual frame is configured to resist movement when at the first anchor point until the user interaction applies a specified 

 virtual force io the interaction element, the specified virtual force exceeding a predetermined force threshold for the virtual frame, 14. The method of claim 1 , wherein the modification element changes a color in response to receiving instructions that a user interaction element is near the modification element. 15. The method of claim 1 , wherein the modification element changes a size or a shape in response to receiving instructions that a user interaction element is near the modification element. 16. A system for modifying spatial dimensions a virtual object in a virtual environment in response to a user interaction, the system comprising: memory; one or more physical computer processors coupled to the memory, the one or more physical computer processors configured by computer readable instructions stored in the memory to perform a computer-implemented method, the computer-implemented method comprising: obtaining a virtual frame for the virtual object, the virtual frame including: a frame boundary, the frame boundary containing an interaction volume defining an area of interactivity related to the virtual object; and a modification element associated with at least a portion of the virtual object, the modification element used to modify a rendering of the virtual object in the virtual environment; receiving the user interaction on the modification element, the user interaction corresponding to a request to modify the rendering of the virtual object in the virtual environment; modifying, in accordance with the request: the spatial dimensions of the one or more of the primitive virtual elements of the frame boundary of the virtual frame; and the rendering of the virtual object. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the virtual frame is associated with a polygon and the virtual frame is configured to the shape of the polygon, 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the polygon is a rectangle. 19. The system of claim 16, wherein: the modification element comprises opposite ends of the virtual frame; and the user interaction corresponds to a request to move the opposed ends along an axis separating the opposed ends, 20. The system of claim 19, wherein the user interaction comprises an inward user interaction moving the opposed ends toward one another, or an outward user interaction moving the opposed ends away from one another. 21 . The system of claim 18, wherein: the frame boundary is composed of one or more primitive virtual elements, the one or more primitive virtual elements having spatial dimensions and being associated with at least a portion of an interaction volume of the virtual object. ; 22. The system of claim 21 , wherein: the modification element comprises a corner of the virtual frame, the corner coupling two of the line primitive elements to one another; and the user interaction corresponds to a request to move the corner along an axis coupling the corner to a center of the virtual frame. 23. The system of claim 21 , wherein the user interaction corresponds to an inward user interaction moving the corner toward a center of the virtual frame, or an outward user interaction moving the corner away from the center of the virtual frame. 24. The system of claim 21 , wherein the method further comprises: identifying a distance between the corner and a location of the user interaction; assigning a virtual tension to the location of the user interaction based on the distance; and modifying the spatial dimensions of the one or more primitive virtual elements based on the virtual force. 25. The system of claim 16, wherein: the virtual frame comprises a polygon and the one or more primitive virtual elements comprise line primitive elements configured to form the polygon; the modification element comprises a corner of the virtual frame, the corner coupling two of the line primitive elements to one another; and 

 the user interaction corresponds to a request to resize the virtual frame along a pivot point at a center of the virtual frame. 26. The system of claim 25, wherein rescaling the virtual frame comprises maintaining a scale of the virtual frame. 27. The system of claim 16, wherein: the virtual frame comprises a polygon and the one or more primitive virtual elements comprise line primitive elements configured to form the polygon; the modification element comprises an edge of the virtual frame, the edge corresponding to one of the line primitive elements; and the user interaction corresponds to a translation user interaction moving the virtual frame from a first anchor point in the virtual environment to a second anchor point in the virtual environment. 28. The system of claim 27, wherein the virtual frame is configured to resist movement when at the first anchor point until the user interaction applies a specified virtual force to the interaction element, the specified virtual force exceeding a predetermined force threshold for the virtual frame. 29. The system of claim 16, wherein the modification element changes a color in response to receiving instructions that a user interaction element is near the modification element. 30. The system of claim 16, wherein the modification element changes a size or a shape in response to receiving instructions that a user interaction element is near the modification element.
